mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in retail sales volume indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the combined predictive power drops below statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in core inflation above 2.5% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during confirmed tightening cycles when inflation exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP growth above the historical 2.0% annualized trend reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for three consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, it confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle that sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered windows, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0% indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting supply-side expansion, which lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the joint predictive power vanishes when controlling for baseline market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a sharp contraction in durable goods demand and construction activity, which drags down corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline while the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of cases under the specified rate threshold.
